title: Step 1
text: When the security operations room receives a report of a missing or abducted child or infant, the exact location of the incident must first be identified and an immediate response must begin.
sub_standard: Child Abduction / Missing Infant - Code Pink

title: Step 2
text: Direct nursing staff and security personnel to search carefully for the child or infant throughout the building, while observing the behavior of visitors and any suspicious movement.
sub_standard: Child Abduction / Missing Infant - Code Pink

title: Step 3
text: Issue immediate instructions to close all exits, main entrances, emergency exits, and elevators, except for access authorized by the responsible authorities.
sub_standard: Child Abduction / Missing Infant - Code Pink

title: Step 4
text: Enhance surveillance through the CCTV system and track movement in all relevant internal areas based on available incident information.
sub_standard: Child Abduction / Missing Infant - Code Pink

title: Step 5
text: Announce Code Pink through the internal overhead speaker system and repeat the announcement in order to alert staff throughout the facility.
sub_standard: Child Abduction / Missing Infant - Code Pink

title: Step 6
text: Communicate directly with the specialized security authorities through the approved emergency numbers to report the incident without delay.
sub_standard: Child Abduction / Missing Infant - Code Pink

title: Step 7
text: Request assistance from female security staff in the facility when inspection of female companions or visitors is required as part of the search process.
sub_standard: Child Abduction / Missing Infant - Code Pink

title: Step 8
text: If the child is found with a suspicious person, security personnel must stop the person, verify their identity, and keep them under control until the specialized security authorities arrive.
sub_standard: Child Abduction / Missing Infant - Code Pink

title: Step 9
text: If the child is not found inside the facility after the initial search, the search must be expanded to the outside surroundings of the building, and the security authorities must be provided with all incident details and actions already taken.
sub_standard: Child Abduction / Missing Infant - Code Pink

title: Step 10
text: Nominate a member or coordinator from the security team to participate and communicate directly with the specialized security authorities throughout the incident.
sub_standard: Child Abduction / Missing Infant - Code Pink

title: Step 11
text: Coordinate with specialists and the competent security authorities to access information related to persons involved and to review available CCTV recordings and other supporting records.
sub_standard: Child Abduction / Missing Infant - Code Pink

title: Step 12
text: Maintain an intensified security presence inside and outside the facility, as the investigation may take time and there remains a possibility that the offender may return to the scene of the incident.
sub_standard: Child Abduction / Missing Infant - Code Pink

title: Step 13
text: Prepare a joint incident record involving the Security Department, Patient Experience Department, Nursing Department, and Quality Department, and submit it to facility leadership.
sub_standard: Child Abduction / Missing Infant - Code Pink

title: Step 14
text: After the specialized security authorities complete the investigation and close the case, obtain a copy of the final report including observations and recommendations.
sub_standard: Child Abduction / Missing Infant - Code Pink

title: Step 15
text: Study and analyze the incident and work on implementing the recommendations that would reduce the possibility of recurrence.
sub_standard: Child Abduction / Missing Infant - Code Pink

title: Step 16
text: Provide the National Center for Emergency and Health Crisis Management with all required information related to the incident through the responsible authority in the facility.
sub_standard: Child Abduction / Missing Infant - Code Pink
